title: Address to Joint Session of Congress 2025
speaker: Donald Trump

segments.text: Thank you very much. This is a great honor. We've accomplished more in 43 days than most administrations accomplish in four years, maybe even eight years.
America is back, open for business, and stronger than ever before. We will very quickly make America greater than ever before.
When I took office, we inherited a mess. We inherited an economic catastrophe and an inflation nightmare.

claims.quote: We inherited an economic catastrophe and an inflation nightmare
claims.actual: BEA/BLS: The economy grew 2.5%+ annually since 2021. Unemployment was 4.0% in Jan 2025, well below historical norms. Inflation had declined from 9.1% peak to ~3% by inauguration.
claims.explanation: Economy was growing steadily with low unemployment when Trump took office.

segments.text: We suffered the worst inflation in 48 years, but perhaps even in the history of our country. Americans saw their grocery bills skyrocket and everything go through the roof.

claims.quote: Worst inflation in 48 years, but perhaps even in the history of our country
claims.actual: BLS CPI: Peak was 9.1% in June 2022 — the highest since November 1981 (41 years, not 48). Inflation exceeded 12-15% in the 1970s-80s and 18% in 1946.
claims.explanation: Inflation was high but far from the worst in U.S. history.

segments.text: Joe Biden, especially, let the price of eggs get out of control. Everyone knows it. Under my leadership, we will bring the price of eggs and everything else back down.

claims.quote: Joe Biden let the price of eggs get out of control
claims.actual: BLS/USDA: Egg prices spiked due to bird flu killing 100+ million chickens — a supply shock, not policy. Egg prices are higher under Trump than Biden's peak, rising ~$2 since inauguration.
claims.explanation: Bird flu drove egg prices; they actually rose further under Trump.

segments.text: We have more liquid gold under our feet than any nation on earth, and by far. We are going to use it. Drill, baby, drill.

claims.quote: We have more liquid gold under our feet than any nation on earth
claims.actual: EIA/OPEC: U.S. ranks 9th-11th globally in proven crude oil reserves behind Venezuela, Saudi Arabia, Iran, Canada, Iraq, and others. U.S. leads in production but not reserves.
claims.explanation: U.S. is a top producer but does not have the most oil reserves.

segments.text: The previous administration closed more than 100 power plants. They were shutting down our energy. We are now opening them back up.

claims.actual: EIA: Total U.S. electric power plants increased by 2,187 from 2020–2023. Coal plants decreased by 57, petroleum by 23. Renewable and natural gas plants far offset closures.
claims.explanation: Some fossil fuel plants closed but total plants increased significantly.

segments.text: We are taking on waste, fraud, and abuse in the federal government. We found hundreds of billions of dollars of fraud. DOGE is doing an incredible job saving taxpayer money.

claims.quote: We found hundreds of billions of dollars of fraud
claims.actual: DOGE website: Claimed $105B in estimated savings as of March 2025. Only $19.8B documented on their 'wall of receipts.' Most identified items were program cuts, not proven fraud.
claims.explanation: Documented savings are far less than claimed; 'fraud' conflated with spending cuts.

segments.text: DOGE found that we were paying $8 billion a year for buildings that are mostly empty. Government employees will come back to work or they will be dismissed.

claims.quote: Paying $8 billion a year for buildings that are mostly empty
claims.actual: GSA/GAO: Federal government spends ~$6-8B annually on leased office space. GAO confirmed many federal buildings were underutilized post-COVID, with some at 25% occupancy.
claims.explanation: Spending figure is roughly accurate; utilization was genuinely low.

segments.text: On April 2nd, reciprocal tariffs kick in. We will take in trillions and trillions of dollars that create jobs like we have never seen before.

claims.quote: We will take in trillions and trillions of dollars
claims.actual: Tax Foundation: Estimated first-year tariff revenue of ~$140B. CBO: Total customs duties in FY2024 were $77B. Tariff costs are largely passed to U.S. consumers.
claims.explanation: Tariff revenue is in the hundreds of billions at most, not trillions.

segments.text: I did it with China, and nobody thought that was possible. We took in hundreds of billions of dollars from China. They paid dearly, not us.

claims.quote: We took in hundreds of billions of dollars from China. They paid, not us
claims.actual: Multiple studies (NBER, Fed, Tax Foundation): U.S. importers and consumers bore nearly all tariff costs. Total China tariff revenue was ~$80B/year, not hundreds of billions.
claims.explanation: Research consistently shows tariff costs are borne by U.S. businesses and consumers.

segments.text: Auto plants are opening up all over the place. They're coming back to Michigan, they're coming back to South Carolina, Ohio, and many other states.

claims.quote: Auto plants are opening up all over the place
claims.actual: BLS: Manufacturing jobs fell by 49,000 since January 2025. While some auto investment announcements were made, most were planned before Trump took office or are conditional.
claims.explanation: Some announcements exist but manufacturing jobs have declined overall.

segments.text: Before the end of my first full business day, we secured nearly $3 trillion of new investments in the United States. SoftBank committed $200 billion. Apple committed $500 billion. TSMC is investing $165 billion.

claims.quote: We secured nearly $3 trillion of new investments
claims.actual: White House tracker: Investment pledges are announcements, not deployed capital. Many were planned before Trump took office. Economists note pledges frequently don't materialize in full.
claims.explanation: These are pledges and announcements, not actual deployed investments.

claims.actual: Apple announced a $500B five-year U.S. spending plan in Feb 2025, including a Houston AI server factory. However, Apple already spends ~$80B/year in the U.S., making this partly a repackaging of existing plans.
claims.explanation: Apple confirmed the pledge, but much reflects continuation of existing spending.

segments.text: We're going to have no tax on tips, no tax on overtime, and no tax on Social Security. People on Social Security have been treated very unfairly.

claims.quote: No tax on tips, no tax on overtime, and no tax on Social Security
claims.actual: Congress: These are proposals requiring legislation. CBO has not scored the combined revenue impact. Estimates suggest $1.5-2T in revenue loss over 10 years if all three pass.
claims.explanation: Campaign promises requiring Congressional action — not yet law.

segments.text: I brought back the TikTok ban, saved it, and now we're going to get a deal where the United States gets 50 percent of TikTok.
The previous administration spent perhaps $350 billion on Ukraine, like taking candy from a baby. And we never got a thank you.

claims.actual: Congressional Research Service: Congress appropriated $174.2B for Ukraine since 2022. Ukraine Oversight Inspector General reported $183B allocated as of Sept 2024.
claims.explanation: Actual amount is roughly half what Trump claimed.

segments.text: I ended the last administration's insane electric vehicle mandate. You can buy any car you want. You can buy a gasoline-powered car, a hybrid, electric, anything.

claims.quote: I ended the last administration's insane electric vehicle mandate
claims.actual: EPA/DOT: The Biden administration never imposed an EV mandate. EPA set tailpipe emission standards; automakers could meet them with any technology mix including hybrids.
claims.explanation: No EV mandate existed — emission standards allowed multiple compliance paths.

segments.text: I always said the stock market is the ultimate report card. And our stock market since Election Day has been doing very well. Trillions of dollars of wealth created.

claims.quote: Our stock market since Election Day has been doing very well. Trillions of dollars of wealth created
claims.actual: S&P 500 rose ~4% from Election Day (Nov 5, 2024) to inauguration. However, markets had been rising under Biden too, with S&P 500 up ~50% from Jan 2021 to Jan 2025.
claims.explanation: Markets rose post-election but context matters — they rose under Biden too.

segments.text: Gas prices are coming down and coming down rapidly. In some states, you can already buy gas for under two dollars a gallon.

claims.quote: In some states, you can already buy gas for under two dollars a gallon
claims.actual: AAA: No state average was below $2/gallon in March 2025. Some individual gas stations may have had promotional prices, but statewide averages ranged from $2.50-$4.00+.
claims.explanation: Individual stations may hit $1.99 but no state average was that low.

segments.text: We are going to make the Trump tax cuts permanent. If we don't, you will have the biggest tax increase in the history of our country.

claims.quote: If we don't, you will have the biggest tax increase in the history of our country
claims.actual: CBO/Tax Policy Center: The 2017 TCJA individual provisions expire after 2025. If not extended, taxes would increase by ~$4 trillion over 10 years — one of the largest increases by value, though not as a share of GDP.
claims.explanation: Expiration would be a large tax increase in dollar terms.

segments.text: We will rebuild our military, take care of our veterans, and always put America first. We are respected again all over the world.
Our country is going to be bigger, better, and stronger than ever before. The best is yet to come. God bless you, and God bless America.
